criteria.name: headings_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings such as Dare Night and section titles, capitalization follows one consistent convention.
criteria.reasoning: Headings consistently use sentence-style capitalization across setup and play screens. Evidence: writing-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders actions, labels are specific verbs such as Draw card, Start game, Undo last turn, Export Session, Save Progress, and Resume Saved Session rather than generic labels.
criteria.reasoning: Primary actions use clear labels such as Start game, Draw card, Undo last turn, Save Progress, Export Session, and Resume-equivalent controls. Evidence: writing-play-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders validation errors, messages name the problem and the fix (including field names name or prompt and length rules).
criteria.reasoning: Validation explicitly identifies the prompt field and required length: “Field prompt must be 8 to 200 characters after trimming.” Evidence: writing-validation-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders the custom-card empty state, copy explains what belongs there and how to add a card.
criteria.reasoning: The custom-card empty state explains that no cards exist and instructs the user to complete the form above to add one. Evidence: writing-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body copy on setup and play, wording is clear and free of obvious typos in primary chrome.
criteria.reasoning: Setup and play copy is clear and free of obvious typos in the observed primary chrome. Evidence: writing-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: terminology_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app names game concepts, it consistently uses Dare Night, points, forfeits, and Winner rather than swapping synonyms in primary chrome.
criteria.reasoning: Observed terminology consistently uses Dare Night, points, and Winner-oriented scoring language without conflicting synonyms. Evidence: writing-play-shared.png

criteria.name: numbers_and_targets_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app shows the win target and scores, First to 10 and point totals use consistent numeric wording.
criteria.reasoning: The interface consistently shows “First to 10,” “Race to 10,” and scores such as “0/10” and “0 of 10 points.” Evidence: writing-play-shared.png

criteria.name: success_messages_specific
criteria.description: Where the app shows toasts such as Deck reshuffled, Saved, or Copied, the messages are specific to the action.
criteria.reasoning: The observed toast is specific: “Custom card added to the deck.” Evidence: writing-success-shared.png

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_visible
criteria.description: Where the app renders UI copy, placeholder lorem/TODO text is visible in shipped screens.
criteria.reasoning: No lorem ipsum, TODO, or other placeholder copy was visible in the observed screens. Evidence: writing-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_errors
criteria.description: Where the app renders prominent headings or primary buttons, obvious spelling errors are present.
criteria.reasoning: No obvious spelling errors appeared in prominent headings or primary buttons. Evidence: writing-play-shared.png

criteria.name: concept_names_inconsistent
criteria.description: Where the app names the same control, labels flip between inconsistent names for the same action across screens.
criteria.reasoning: Repeated controls and game concepts retained consistent names across setup and play views. Evidence: writing-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: copy_truncated_or_overflowing
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels on pills and chips, text is truncated or overflowing in a way that hides the action name.
criteria.reasoning: Labels and chips were visibly complete; read-only measurement found no overflowing action labels. Evidence: writing-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: writing_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in writing that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant browser-observable writing defect was found beyond the covered criteria. Evidence: writing-initial-shared.png
